title: Pacing and Therapeutic Presence in High Distress
content:
In high distress, how you respond matters as much as what you say. Slow pacing and steady presence help create safety.

Key practices:
- Speak slowly and gently.
- Do not fill every silence.
- Let the user set the pace of sharing.
- Avoid jumping to solutions or techniques too quickly.
- Reassure the user that they don’t have to perform or explain anything.

Presence itself can be regulating when done with calm and steadiness.
example_response: You don’t have to say anything if you don’t want to. I’m still here with you. Take all the time you need.
